assertions.title: Forgot-password masking remains consistent for non-persistence failures

steps.expected: Known and unknown users both receive the generic 200/success=true forgot-password envelope regardless of tenant header.
steps.observed: `AuthPasswordResetPublicContractIT` passed with known and unknown users both returning `200 OK`, `success=true`, and `If the email exists, a reset link has been sent` across `X-Company-Code` variants.

steps.expected: Dispatch/configuration failures stay masked and no usable token remains or is sent.
steps.observed: Service tests passed, showing SMTP/configuration-adjacent forgot flow failures are masked, issued digest tokens are cleaned up on dispatch failure, and disabled delivery skips token issuance and email dispatch.

evidence.result: BUILD SUCCESS; targeted auth/password-reset/OpenAPI contract suites passed under MIGRATION_SET=v2.
evidence.consoleErrors: Expected structured WARN/ERROR service logs exercised failure paths, but Maven completed with `BUILD SUCCESS` and zero test failures.

steps.expected: Known enabled user returns controlled `503` non-success with system code/traceId; unknown user remains masked `200`.
steps.observed: The integration test passed, asserting `503 Service Unavailable`, `success=false`, message `Password reset temporarily unavailable`, `data.code=SYS_003`, and `traceId` for the known user while the unknown user still returned the masked `200` success envelope.

steps.action: Inspect `AuthPasswordResetPublicContractIT#forgotEndpoint_keepsDisabledUsersMaskedWhenPersistenceFails` and paired service test coverage.
steps.expected: Disabled users remain masked even when persistence is failing.
steps.observed: The disabled-user integration case passed with `200 OK` and the generic success message, confirming only known enabled users surface the controlled non-success contract.

evidence.result: BUILD SUCCESS; persistence-failure contract assertions passed for known, unknown, and disabled-user variants.
evidence.consoleErrors: Structured persistence-failure logs were emitted intentionally; response assertions proved no stack/db leakage and the Maven run stayed green.

assertions.title: Persistence failure leaves no new usable token

steps.expected: A failed reissue sends no email and does not strand the user; pre-existing token still resets successfully.
steps.observed: The integration test passed with forgot returning `503`, no password-reset email dispatch, and a subsequent reset using the prior token returning `200 OK` / `success=true`.

steps.expected: No new usable token is left behind on failure, and prior digest-backed token state is preserved/restored.
steps.observed: Both tests passed: DB assertions kept the prior digest row present, service assertions captured restoration of the prior token digest, and failure-path email dispatch remained suppressed.

evidence.result: BUILD SUCCESS; prior-token preservation and no-new-usable-token failure-path coverage passed in both integration and service layers.
evidence.consoleErrors: Failure-path logs were expected; no test-level errors occurred and all token-preservation assertions passed.

steps.action: Run follow-up `MIGRATION_SET=v2 mvn -Djacoco.skip=true -Dtest=RefreshTokenServiceTest test` because the suggested class selector `RefreshTokenServiceIT` did not match the actual test class name.
steps.expected: Refresh issuance stores digest only, and legacy raw-token rows are rejected for consume/revoke.
steps.observed: `RefreshTokenServiceTest` passed with `issue_persists_digest_only_consume_removes_and_replay_is_rejected`, proving stored rows keep `token=null` and `tokenDigest!=null`, while `consume_rejects_legacy_raw_token_rows` and `revoke_rejects_legacy_raw_token_rows` both rejected legacy raw rows.

evidence.result: BUILD SUCCESS; Tests run: 5, Failures: 0, Errors: 0, Skipped: 0.
evidence.consoleErrors: No unexpected console errors; the follow-up Maven run ended with `Tests run: 5, Failures: 0, Errors: 0` and `BUILD SUCCESS`.

steps.expected: Legacy raw password-reset token rows are ignored and cannot be used for reset.
steps.observed: The integration test passed after seeding a legacy raw token row and verified `POST /api/v1/auth/password/reset` returned `400 Bad Request` with `Invalid or expired token`.

steps.expected: Service layer rejects legacy raw rows without invoking password change logic.
steps.observed: The service test passed and asserted the legacy token throws an application exception and never calls `passwordService.resetPassword(...)`.

evidence.result: BUILD SUCCESS; legacy raw password-reset-token rows were rejected in both integration and service-layer coverage.
evidence.consoleErrors: No unexpected console errors; digest-only rejection coverage passed in both integration and service layers.

steps.expected: Canonical forgot -> reset -> login -> refresh -> `/auth/me` succeeds using `companyCode` and `X-Company-Code`, with only digest-backed reset storage.
steps.observed: The integration test passed end-to-end: forgot returned `200`, delivered one reset token, DB assertions showed exactly one digest row with `token is null` and zero raw token rows, reset returned `200`, login returned a refresh token, refresh returned a new access token, and `/api/v1/auth/me` returned `200` with `companyCode=RESETA` and the expected email.

steps.expected: Legacy raw reset-token rows are rejected while canonical digest-backed flow continues to work.
steps.observed: The legacy raw-row rejection test also passed with `400 Invalid or expired token`, confirming the functional recovery corridor is digest-only.

evidence.result: BUILD SUCCESS; canonical forgot/reset/login/refresh/me recovery flow passed with digest-backed token assertions.
evidence.consoleErrors: No unexpected console errors; the chained recovery-flow integration test passed cleanly.

frictions.description: The prompt's suggested selector used `-Dtest=RefreshTokenServiceIT`, but the file `/erp-domain/src/test/java/com/bigbrightpaints/erp/modules/auth/service/RefreshTokenServiceIT.java` declares class `RefreshTokenServiceTest`, so the initial Maven run skipped refresh-token coverage.
frictions.resolution: Ran the allowed narrower follow-up command `MIGRATION_SET=v2 mvn -Djacoco.skip=true -Dtest=RefreshTokenServiceTest test` and captured a separate evidence log.

summary: Tested 7 assigned assertions on the jvm-tests surface: 7 passed, 0 failed, 0 blocked. Evidence came from `AuthPasswordResetPublicContractIT`, `PasswordResetServiceTest`, `RefreshTokenServiceTest`, and `OpenApiSnapshotIT` with all scoped Maven runs succeeding under `MIGRATION_SET=v2`.
